src/shared/ui/accordion.tsx\u000099c1670.title: React 19 API migration can break callers

src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.title: Data passed to parent via effect
src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.message: Handing data back to a parent from a useEffect costs your users an extra render.
src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.help: Fetch the data in the parent and pass it down as a prop (or return it from the hook), instead of handing it back up through a prop callback in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#passing-data-to-the-parent

src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.title: Parent kept in sync with a callback effect
src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.message: Your parent re-renders on every local state change because this useEffect calls the prop "setApi" just to stay in sync.
src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.help: Move the shared state into a Provider so both sides read the same value. Then you don't need a useEffect to keep them in sync.

src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.title: Listener re-subscribes on every handler change
src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.message: useEffect re-adds the "onSelect" listener every time the handler changes.
src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.help: Store the handler in a ref and have the listener read `handlerRef.current()`. The subscription stays put while the latest handler still runs.

src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.title: Unstable context provider value

src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u0000409fddcc.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

src/pages/AuthPage.tsx\u00004b6fdd771d7b.help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: State synced to a prop inside an effect

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: All state reset on prop change
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.message: Your users briefly see stale state when a prop changes because this useEffect clears all state.
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Pass the prop as `key` so React resets the component for you when the prop changes, instead of clearing every state value by hand in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#resetting-all-state-when-a-prop-changes

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: Control missing accessible label
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.message: Blind users can't tell what this control does because screen readers find no label, so add visible text, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Give every interactive control a label screen readers can read.

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: Autofocus on an element
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.message: `autoFocus` moves focus on load, which can disrupt screen reader and keyboard users. Remove it and let users choose where to focus.
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Do not use `autoFocus`. It disorients users on load.

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: Button missing explicit type
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.message: Your users can submit the form by accident because a `<button>` with no `type` defaults to submit.
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Set an explicit button `type` so plain buttons do not submit forms by accident: `type="button"`, `"submit"`, or `"reset"`.

src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onClose" changes, even though it's only used inside `addEventListener`.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onClose" changes, even though it's only used inside `addEventListener`.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.title: State synced to a prop inside an effect
src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.message: This effect adjusts state after a prop changes, so users briefly see the stale value.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TextModelSe.help: Adjust the state inline during render with a `prev`-prop comparison (`if (prop !== prevProp) { setPrevProp(prop); setX(...); }`), or refactor to remove the duplicated state. Routing the adjustment through a useEffect forces an extra render with a stale UI between the two commits. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#adjusting-some-state-when-a-prop-changes

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Prop mirrored into state via effect
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.message: Your screen shows the old value first because useState "activeProvider" copies prop "selectedProviderId" through this effect.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.help: Delete both the `useState` and the `useEffect` and read the prop directly while rendering. Copying a prop into state shows the old value on the first render before the effect catches up.

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Prop derived into useState
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.message: Your users see a stale value when prop "selectedProviderId" changes because useState copies it once.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.help: Compute the value inline so prop changes do not leave `useState` holding a stale copy.

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Derived value copied into state
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.message: Storing "activeProvider" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Derived state stored in an effect
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.message: You pay an extra render for state you can derive from other values.
src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.help: Work out derived values while rendering: `const x = fn(dep)`. To reset a component's state when a prop changes, give it a key prop: `<Component key={prop} />`. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect

src/features/generation-queue/ui/QueueTo.title: Parent kept in sync with a callback effect

src/features/generation-queue/ui/QueueTo.help: Move the shared state into a Provider so both sides read the same value. Then you don't need a useEffect to keep them in sync.

src/features/generation-queue/model/Queu.title: React 19 API migration can break callers
src/features/generation-queue/model/Queu.message: useContext is replaced by `use()` in React 19, which reads context inside ifs & loops too, so switch to `import { use } from 'react'`.
src/features/generation-queue/model/Queu.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

src/features/copy-toast/model/CopyToast..title: Full Framer Motion import
src/features/copy-toast/model/CopyToast..message: Importing "motion" ships about 30 kb of extra code and slows page load. Use "m" with LazyMotion instead.
src/features/copy-toast/model/CopyToast..help: Use `import { LazyMotion, m } from "framer-motion"` with `domAnimation` features. Saves about 30kb.

src/features/command-palette/model/useCo.title: React 19 API migration can break callers

src/features/command-palette/model/useCo.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

src/features/command-palette/model/useCo.title: Unstable context provider value
src/features/command-palette/model/useCo.message: Every reader of this context redraws on each render because you build its `value` inline.
src/features/command-palette/model/useCo.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

src/components/ModelCard.tsx\u00009b40ca.title: Static value rebuilt every render
src/components/ModelCard.tsx\u00009b40ca.message: `routeMap` inside `ModelCard` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
src/components/ModelCard.tsx\u00009b40ca.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

src/components/NavLink.tsx\u0000ea50f9c8.title: React 19 API migration can break callers

src/components/NavLink.tsx\u0000ea50f9c8.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

src/components/workspace/PromptBlock.tsx.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
src/components/workspace/PromptBlock.tsx.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onClose" changes, even though it's only used inside `addEventListener`.
src/components/workspace/PromptBlock.tsx.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

src/features/generation-queue/model/Queu.help: Pass `ref` as a normal prop on function components, since `forwardRef` isn't needed in React 19. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)`. Only runs on React 19+ projects.

src/shared/ui/carousel.tsx\u000068899988.title: React 19 API migration can break callers

src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.title: flushSync skips View Transitions
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.message: `flushSync` forces an immediate update, which skips View Transitions and concurrent rendering.
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.help: flushSync forces an immediate update that skips View Transitions and concurrent rendering. Use startTransition for updates that are not urgent.

src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.title: Effects chained together
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.message: Your screen redraws several times from a single action because one useEffect changes "prompt", which sets off this one.
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.help: Compute as much as possible during render (e.g. `const isGameOver = round > 5`) and write all related state inside the event handler that originally fires the chain. Each effect link adds an extra render and makes the code rigid as requirements evolve

src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.title: Many related useState calls
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.message: 11 useState calls in "VideoPage" can each trigger a separate render.
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.title: Event logic handled in an effect
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.message: Faking an event handler with state plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.title: State initialized from a mount effect
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.message: Your users see an extra render with empty "prompt" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
src/pages/VideoPage.tsx\u00002e673cba378.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "onClose" changes, even though it's only used inside `addEventListener`.
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.title: State synced to a prop inside an effect
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.message: This effect adjusts state after a prop changes, so users briefly see the stale value.
src/features/model-picker/ui/ModelSelect.help: Adjust the state inline during render with a `prev`-prop comparison (`if (prop !== prevProp) { setPrevProp(prop); setX(...); }`), or refactor to remove the duplicated state. Routing the adjustment through a useEffect forces an extra render with a stale UI between the two commits. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#adjusting-some-state-when-a-prop-changes

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Prop derived into useState

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Derived value copied into state

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.title: Derived state stored in an effect

src/features/model-picker/ui/TwoPanelMod.help: Work out derived values while rendering: `const x = fn(dep)`. To reset a component's state when a prop changes, give it a key prop: `<Component key={prop} />`. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect
